headline: Starbucks to open new corporate office in Nashville
summary: Starbucks said Tuesday it’s opening a corporate office in Nashville, Tennessee, to support its planned U.S. expansion.  Seattle, where Starbucks was founded in 1971, will remain the company’s global headquarters.  In a memo to employees Tuesday, Starbucks’ Chief Partner Officer Sara Kelly said some employees will be new hires and others will be contract workers who will transition to full-time roles with Starbucks.

headline: Newsweek Names Integrity One of America's Greatest Workplaces for Culture, Belonging & Community
summary: Integrity, LLC ("Integrity"), a leading distributor of life and health insurance, and provider of wealth management and retirement planning solutions, today announced it has been recognized by Newsweek as one of America's Greatest Workplaces for Culture, Belonging & Community. This prestigious award honors the company's emphasis on creating an environment where employees feel valued, heard and connected. Other winners include American Express, Google, GE, Starbucks, 3M, Accenture and Adobe.

headline: Keurig Dr Pepper and Nestlé USA Extend Strategic Partnership
summary: Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) and Nestlé USA (Nestlé) today announced an extended agreement renewing and expanding their strategic partnership, including the manufacturing and distribution of Starbucks® K-Cup® pods in the U.S. and Canada.
